items.rationale: Core thesis of his book and career: blockchains/tokens are the only plausible path to an open internet that returns ownership to users and away from Big Tech; he frames the space as still in 'early innings.'

citations.quote: I wrote Read Write Own because I felt we needed a book that explains the full potential of blockchains and tokens
Crypto/web3 is still in the early innings, and needs clear regulations to provide an open pathway for startups to build

items.rationale: His most concrete recent investing conviction: $40M into CCP Games' EVE AAA title and a seed into Proof of Play, betting on composability, player ownership, modding, and autonomous worlds as native blockchain use cases.

citations.quote: I’m thrilled to share that @a16z is leading a $40M investment in @CCPGames’ new AAA gaming project set in the EVE Universe.
Excited to announce that we’re leading a seed investment in @ProofOfPlay, an on-chain game studio and tech company

items.rationale: He treats composability and open source as the structural advantage of crypto-native systems — players/developers own assets and build on top of each other — and a recurring framework in his book and talks.

citations.quote: committed to building a future with composability, interoperability, and permanence at the core of gameplay
composability & open source software

items.rationale: Personally an active Farcaster user and repeatedly highlights its community as superior to incumbent social media; consistent with his vision of user-owned networks replacing extractive platforms.

citations.quote: I use Farcaster all the time. The community is far better than on Twitter.
Some of my favorite mentions from the Farcaster community

items.rationale: Continues to actively promote a forward-looking 'vision for NFTs' on the book tour, treating them as durable infrastructure for ownership rather than a faded fad.

citations.quote: Thanks @msuster for hosting me at Upfront Summit to talk about Read Write Own... my vision for NFTs, and more

items.rationale: Backed Gensyn (decentralized compute) and frames AI as a key intersecting application area in his book; sees crypto rails as relevant to AI, though expressed less frequently than gaming.
items.age_note: AI emphasis appears mainly via the June-2023 Gensyn deal and book framing; not heavily restated in 2024 statements.

citations.quote: today we announced our newest investment in UK-based @gensynai
Sure, timelines in tech are hard to predict. I also thought AI would come sooner.

items.rationale: Expressed enthusiasm that VR would follow the standard computing-platform cost-curve once Apple and Meta invested; a platform-trend bet rather than a crypto thesis.
items.age_note: Stated once on 2023-06-05 (Vision Pro launch) and never restated in the following year; treat as a persisted, aging view.

citations.quote: Excited for Vision Pro. With Meta and now Apple investment, VR will follow the same pattern as past computing platforms

items.rationale: His single most-repeated concern: without comprehensive legislation (FIT21, SAB121 repeal) well-intentioned US startups face crippling compliance costs and bad actors thrive; intensified through mid-2024 with the SAB121 veto.

citations.quote: It’s more clear than ever that we need to get comprehensive crypto legislation passed into law this year.
an important step in providing long-awaited regulatory clarity for crypto, protecting U.S. consumers, and promoting American innovation

items.rationale: Repeatedly disavows the speculative, casino culture around crypto as harmful to public perception and to the technology's productive potential — a dedicated part of his book is devoted to it.

citations.quote: stamping out the harmful “casino” culture that has developed around crypto
the harmful casino culture that has developed around blockchains that hurts their public perception and undermines their potential

items.rationale: His most recent pointed critique: current US rules let only memecoins flourish while companies and tokens with productive uses face hurdles — backward incentives that need a regulatory pathway to fix.

citations.quote: the backward incentives of a regulatory regime in the US that lets only memecoins thrive — while companies and tokens with more productive uses face hurdles
Yes, but you need a regulatory pathway to expand it beyond a toy

items.rationale: Frames the strategic stakes as wresting the internet's future away from Big Tech, and sees big-bank and big-tech lobbies as the political force crypto must counter to survive.

citations.quote: the only way to counteract the lobbies of the big banks and big tech is to show that crypto and blockchain can be a force, too

items.rationale: He neither defends nor diminishes memecoins per se, but is concerned they are the only token category the current US regime lets thrive, crowding out more productive tokens; he views them as a 'toy' absent a regulatory pathway.

citations.quote: My goal isn't to defend or to diminish memecoins... a regulatory regime in the US that lets only memecoins thrive
Yes, but you need a regulatory pathway to expand it beyond a toy

risk_regime.rationale: Dixon is structurally and durably bullish on crypto/web3, describing it as 'early innings,' publishing a manifesto, expanding a16z internationally, and deploying large checks into gaming and infra — all long-horizon conviction bets. His risk-on posture is explicitly conditioned on regulatory clarity and a rejection of short-term speculative 'casino' froth, so it is builder-optimism rather than indiscriminate market bullishness.

notes: View is heavily structural/long-horizon and centered on his 2024 book Read Write Own. Strongest, most concrete excitement is web3 gaming/virtual worlds (CCP, Proof of Play) and decentralized social (Farcaster, used personally). No individual fungible tokens are named as buys — he discusses tokens as a category, so tokens_excited is empty by evidence. The dominant 2024 theme is policy: regulatory clarity is simultaneously his biggest concern and the precondition for his risk-on stance, sharpening notably from May–June 2024 (FIT21 passage, SAB121 veto). The memecoin critique (April 2024) is his most recent token-specific statement. VR/Vision Pro is an aging one-off (2023-06-05) outside his crypto core and flagged as persisted.
